title: AWS and OpenAI Announce Expanded Partnership Bringing Frontier AI to Amazon Bedrock
summary: OpenAI's models, Codex agent, and a new managed agent platform are now available in limited preview on AWS's Amazon Bedrock infrastructure.
content: Amazon Web Services and OpenAI announced a major expansion of their partnership on April 28, 2026, making OpenAI's frontier models available through Amazon Bedrock alongside models from Anthropic, Meta, and others. The partnership introduces three offerings in limited preview: OpenAI models on Bedrock with AWS's full enterprise security controls, Codex on Bedrock bringing OpenAI's coding agent to the more than 4 million weekly users within AWS environments, and Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI for building production-ready agentic applications. The announcement came just one day after Microsoft and OpenAI restructured their exclusive arrangement, freeing OpenAI to serve customers across any cloud provider. AWS CEO Matt Garman said at the launch event, 'This is what our customers have been asking us for for a really long time.' OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, appearing via recorded message, emphasized that the partnership brings together OpenAI's frontier intelligence with the infrastructure enterprises already trust.

title: Google Grants Pentagon Broad AI Access After Anthropic's Refusal
summary: Google has agreed to give the U.S. Department of Defense access to its AI for classified networks, becoming the third major AI company to do so after Anthropic publicly refused similar terms.
content: Google has granted the U.S. Department of Defense access to its AI for use on classified networks, allowing essentially all lawful uses, according to multiple news reports from April 28, 2026. The deal follows Anthropic's high-profile refusal to grant the Pentagon unrestricted AI access without guardrails against domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons use — a stance that led the DoD to brand Anthropic a 'supply-chain risk,' a designation normally reserved for foreign adversaries. Google became the third AI company to capitalize on Anthropic's position, joining OpenAI and xAI, though its agreement includes non-binding language stating the intent is not to use AI for domestic mass surveillance or autonomous weapons. The deal was struck despite 950 Google employees signing an open letter urging the company to follow Anthropic's lead and insist on similar safeguards. An ongoing lawsuit between Anthropic and the DoD continues, with a judge having granted Anthropic an injunction against the 'supply-chain risk' designation while the case proceeds.

title: Microsoft and OpenAI Restructure Partnership, Ending Exclusivity and Capping Revenue Share
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have amended their foundational agreement, making OpenAI's cloud relationship non-exclusive and allowing it to serve customers across any cloud provider.
content: Microsoft and OpenAI announced a major restructuring of their partnership on April 27, 2026, amending terms that have defined the AI industry since Microsoft's multi-billion dollar investment in the company. Under the new agreement, OpenAI can now serve all its products to customers across any cloud provider rather than being tied exclusively to Azure, while Microsoft remains the primary partner and retains first-ship rights unless it cannot support necessary capabilities. Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI, while OpenAI's own revenue share payments to Microsoft continue through 2030 at the same percentage but subject to a new total cap. Microsoft's license to OpenAI IP for models and products, which extends through 2032, becomes non-exclusive, freeing OpenAI to license its technology more broadly. The amendment came the day before OpenAI formally announced its expanded partnership with Amazon Web Services, marking a fundamental shift in the competitive cloud AI landscape.

title: Ex-DeepMind Researcher David Silver Raises $1.1B to Build AI That Learns Without Human Data
summary: David Silver, creator of AlphaGo and AlphaZero, raised $1.1 billion at a $5.1 billion valuation for Ineffable Intelligence, a months-old British AI lab targeting reinforcement learning-based 'superlearner' systems.
content: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded just months ago by David Silver — the former Google DeepMind researcher who created AlphaGo and AlphaZero — raised $1.1 billion in funding at a $5.1 billion valuation, according to a TechCrunch report on April 27, 2026. The round was led by Sequoia Capital and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with participation from Index Ventures, Google, Nvidia, the British Business Bank, and Sovereign AI, the U.K.'s new sovereign venture fund for AI. Ineffable Intelligence aims to build a 'superlearner' capable of discovering all knowledge and skills through reinforcement learning without relying on human-generated training data — a direct extension of Silver's decade-long research at DeepMind, where AI systems learned games like chess and Go purely through self-play. The $5.1 billion valuation makes it immediately one of Europe's most valuable pre-product AI startups, part of a growing wave of 'coconut round' companies founded by star researchers — including Yann LeCun's AMI Labs and Tim Rocktäschel's Recursive Superintelligence — signaling London's emergence as a major AI hub.

title: UK Launches AI Sovereignty Strategy With New Hardware Plan to Secure National AI Future
summary: UK Technology Secretary Liz Kendall unveiled a landmark AI sovereignty strategy, committing to a domestic AI hardware plan and positioning Britain as an indispensable node in global AI architecture.
content: UK Technology Secretary Liz Kendall delivered a landmark speech on April 28, 2026 at the Royal United Services Institute, announcing a new AI sovereignty strategy designed to reduce Britain's dependencies on foreign technology and secure its competitive position in AI. The strategy emphasizes that 70% of global AI compute is now controlled by just five companies, framing control over AI infrastructure as fundamental to economic, energy, and defense security. Kendall announced a new UK AI Hardware Plan to develop domestic capability in chips and semiconductor technologies underpinning the full AI stack, alongside deeper collaboration with other 'middle power' nations to shape global AI deployment standards. The government's approach centers on becoming 'a keystone in the global AI architecture' by concentrating investment in areas of British competitive advantage — from frontier research and the AI Security Institute to compute and skills infrastructure — rather than attempting to build everything domestically. Kendall rejected calls to slow AI development, calling such a move 'a double betrayal' of British talent and interests, and cited the U.K.'s AI Security Institute as a key institution already shaping international AI safety approaches.

title: IBM Launches Bob, an Enterprise AI Development Partner Covering the Full Software Lifecycle
summary: IBM has made IBM Bob generally available, an AI-first development platform used internally by 80,000 employees who report an average 45% productivity gain across software modernization and development tasks.
content: IBM announced the global availability of IBM Bob on April 28, 2026, an enterprise AI development partner designed to handle the full software development lifecycle from planning and coding through testing, deployment, and modernization. Bob uses multi-model orchestration to automatically route tasks to the most appropriate model — including Anthropic Claude, Mistral open-source models, and IBM Granite — based on accuracy, performance, and cost, with simpler completions routed to lighter models and complex work sent to frontier models. The platform launched internally at IBM in June 2025 with 100 developers and scaled to over 80,000 IBM employees worldwide, who self-report an average 45% productivity gain; one team reported a 70% reduction in time on selected tasks saving 10 hours per week, and consulting firm Blue Pearl completed a typical 30-day Java upgrade in just 3 days using Bob. IBM Bob is now available as a SaaS offering with a 30-day free trial and individual and enterprise plans, with on-premises deployment planned for organizations with data residency or compliance requirements. Enterprise customers including Ernst & Young are already using the platform to accelerate modernization of global-scale systems with embedded governance, security scanning, and full auditability at every step.
